normalized_title: Development Control Policy 1.6 Planning to Support Transit Use and Transit Oriented Development
version_date: January 2006 (amendment of DC 1.6 'Planning To Enhance Public Transport Use' 1999, adopted by the WAPC in 2005)
scope_summary: WAPC policy promoting the integration of land use and public transport in the Perth and Peel region. It defines transit oriented precincts by walkable-catchment thresholds around rail stations, interchanges and high-frequency bus stops, and sets measures for transit-supportive development patterns, land uses (including minimum residential densities in transit precincts), the public domain, transit-supportive design, integration of transit infrastructure, and precinct planning, to be delivered largely through local government town planning schemes and structure planning.

key_numeric_standards.topic: walkable catchment - rail stations and major interchanges
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: definition of transit oriented precincts around rail stations, transit interchanges or major bus transfer stations/terminals
bus stops on routes with multiple services at peak frequency of 15 minutes or less

key_numeric_standards.unit: m (total width, may be somewhat exceeded)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: confined corridor of development centred on a rail station

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum residential density in transit precincts
key_numeric_standards.unit: dwellings per hectare (minimum; substantially higher expected close to rail stations, major bus interchanges or rail-equivalent bus routes)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: residential development opportunities local governments are expected to identify and promote in transit precincts when reviewing town planning schemes

definitions: transit oriented precinct / TOD precinct (defined by walkable catchment thresholds, s1)
ped-shed / walkable catchment (s1: plotting actual walking distances against the street network)

cross_references.cited: Statement of Planning Policy 3 Urban Growth and Settlements (SPP3)
Development Control Policy 1.6 Planning To Enhance Public Transport Use 1999 (predecessor)

quality_flags: missing spaces after commas throughout the extracted text (e.g. 'variable,and', 'time,or') - PDF text-extraction artifact
two-column layout interleaving on several pages (heading and body fragments merged on the same lines)
final page 'Indicative area of application of policy' is a map; little or no extractable text expected for it
manifest instrument_name shortens the title to '...Transit Use and Development'; document title is '...Transit Use and Transit Oriented Development' - same instrument
